summary: Gruber follows up on his previous post about Apple Watch Solo Loop sizing difficulties. Apple updated its PDF sizing tool with clearer instructions, matching his suggestion that users should wrap tightly rather than loosely. He then provides a practical service by mapping existing Sport Band hole positions to Solo Loop sizes, discovering they correspond exactly. He includes photos and a table showing the mappings across all four Sport Band combinations, and documents an off-by-one measurement error caused by using a 44mm watch body with 38/40mm bands.
key_points: Apple updated its Solo Loop sizing instructions to say 'wrap tightly' instead of the ambiguous 'snug but not too tight', matching Gruber's suggestion from the previous day
Sport Band holes map exactly to Solo Loop sizes, providing an alternative sizing method for people who already own an Apple Watch
The mapping covers four combinations: 38/40mm and 42/44mm watches, each with S/M and M/L bands, spanning sizes 1-12
Gruber's initial measurements had an off-by-one bug caused by testing 38/40mm bands on a 44mm watch body, which spreads connectors slightly wider
money_quotes: No more ambiguous 'snug but not too tight'. Now they've made clear that it should wrap tight enough not to slide around.
It's no longer a safe assumption that everyone has access to a printer, either, so let's figure out the mappings here.
It turns out Sport Band holes do map exactly to the new Solo Loop sizes.
My measurements for the smaller 38/40mm Sport Bands had an off-by-one bug when I originally published the photo and table below.
It was really dumb, like any good off-by-one bug.

The range of wrist sizes for the Sport Bands corresponds exactly to the new Solo Loops — the first hole on the 38/40mm S/M Sport Band is a size 1, and the last hole on the 42/44mm M/L Sport Band is a size 12.
key_insight: Apple Watch Sport Band holes map exactly to Solo Loop sizes, providing an alternative sizing method that doesn't require a printer, and Apple's updated instructions now properly emphasize tight wrapping over the previously ambiguous guidance.
